feature: TUI Feature Port from tui_old.rs to Modular src/tui/

user_stories.title: Spawned Sub-Agent Data Structures and State
user_stories.description:
Port the SpawnedAgent struct and all associated state fields from tui_old.rs (L415-L500) into the new modular TUI. This is the foundation for the entire sub-agent system.

In tui_old.rs, SpawnedAgent is a struct with fields: name, instructions, model, session (Session), created_at. The App struct holds: spawned_agents (HashMap<String, SpawnedAgent>), active_spawned_agent (Option<String>), agent_response_rxs (per-agent mpsc receivers), streaming_agent_texts (HashMap<String, String>), agent_tool_started_at.

Add these to AppState in src/tui/app/state.rs. Also add agent_profiles() function that maps profile names to codenames (Strategist, Archivist, Forge, Sentinel, Probe) from tui_old.rs L10900-L10950.
acceptance_criteria: SpawnedAgent struct exists with name, instructions, model, session, created_at fields
agent_profile() function returns codename profiles (Strategist, Archivist, Forge, Sentinel, Probe)

user_stories.title: Spawned Sub-Agent Commands: /spawn, /kill, /agents
user_stories.description:
Implement the slash commands for managing spawned sub-agents. Port from tui_old.rs L4584-L4610 (/spawn), L4774-L4800 (/kill), L4723-L4770 (/agents).

/spawn <name> [instructions] — Creates a new SpawnedAgent with its own Session. Uses agent_profile() to assign a codename if name matches a known profile. Creates a new Session for the agent and inserts into spawned_agents HashMap. Announces creation on the bus if available.

/kill <name> — Removes the agent from spawned_agents. If active_spawned_agent was that agent, clears it. Announces shutdown on bus.

/agents — Lists all spawned agents with their name, model, message count, and active status. Shows protocol status if bus is connected.

Add these to src/tui/app/commands.rs.
acceptance_criteria: /spawn command creates a SpawnedAgent with its own Session
/spawn with known profile name uses agent_profile() codename
/kill removes agent from HashMap and clears active_spawned_agent if needed
/agents lists all agents with name, model, message count
Commands registered in slash command hint list

user_stories.title: Agent Focus and Message Routing
user_stories.description:
Implement agent focus, @mention routing, and the /agent command. Port from tui_old.rs L4720-L4960.

/agent <name> — Focus chat on a specific spawned agent (sets active_spawned_agent). /agent main or /agent off clears focus. /agent <name> <message> sends a single message without changing focus. /agent with no args opens agent picker (ViewMode::AgentPicker).

@mention routing: When input starts with @agent_name, dispatch to that agent. When active_spawned_agent is set, all messages auto-route to that agent.

dispatch_to_agent_internal() — sends message to agent's Session, handles response via agent_response_rxs channel. handle_agent_response() — processes SessionEvent from spawned agent (mirrors handle_response but prefixes with agent name).

Add ViewMode::AgentPicker variant to src/tui/models/mod.rs.
acceptance_criteria: /agent <name> <message> sends one message without changing focus

user_stories.title: Smart Model Switching on Errors
user_stories.description:
Port the smart model switching system from tui_old.rs L10800-L11000. When a provider returns a retryable error, automatically switch to a fallback model and retry.

Key functions:
- is_retryable_provider_error(error_msg) — classifies errors as retryable (rate limit, timeout, 5xx, connection refused, context length exceeded) vs permanent
- smart_switch_preferred_models(provider) — returns ordered fallback model list per provider
- maybe_schedule_smart_switch_retry() — called from session_events Error handler, increments retry count, picks next model
- apply_pending_smart_switch_retry() — executes the retry with the new model

Add fields to AppState: smart_switch_retry_count (u32), smart_switch_attempted_models (Vec<String>), pending_smart_switch_retry (Option<PendingSmartSwitchRetry>).

Constants already exist in src/tui/constants.rs (SMART_SWITCH_MAX_RETRIES, SMART_SWITCH_PROVIDER_PRIORITY). Tests already exist in src/tui/tests.rs.
acceptance_criteria: smart_switch_preferred_models() returns fallback list per provider
On retryable error, automatic retry with next model
Retry count tracked and capped at SMART_SWITCH_MAX_RETRIES

user_stories.title: Watchdog Timer for Stuck Requests
user_stories.description:
Port the watchdog timer from tui_old.rs. The watchdog detects when the main session has been processing for too long without receiving events, and auto-restarts.

Add fields to AppState: main_watchdog_root_prompt (Option<String>), main_last_event_at (Option<Instant>), main_watchdog_restart_count (u32), main_inflight_prompt (Option<String>).

In the event loop (src/tui/app/event_loop.rs), add a tokio::select! branch with a timer that fires every MAIN_PROCESSING_WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_SECS. When fired, check if main_last_event_at exceeds the timeout. If so, cancel the current request and re-send main_watchdog_root_prompt.

Update session_events.rs to set main_last_event_at on every SessionEvent received.
acceptance_criteria: Event loop has timer branch checking for stuck requests

user_stories.title: File Picker UI
user_stories.description:
Port the interactive file picker from tui_old.rs L245-L280 (structs) and render function (~L9600).

Structs: FilePickerEntry with name (String) and kind (FilePickerEntryKind: Parent, Directory, File). State fields: file_picker_dir (PathBuf), file_picker_entries (Vec<FilePickerEntry>), file_picker_selected (usize), file_picker_filter (String).

Add ViewMode::FilePicker variant. Add Ctrl+O key binding to open file picker. open_file_picker() populates entries from filesystem (directories first, then files, with '..' parent entry). attach_file_to_input() inserts selected path into input buffer.

Render function shows directory listing with selection highlight, filter input, and file preview. Navigation: arrow keys select, Enter opens directory or attaches file, Esc cancels, typing filters.

Constants already exist in src/tui/constants.rs (FILE_PICKER_MAX_ENTRIES, FILE_PICKER_PREVIEW_*).

Update /file command to open the picker when called without args (currently just does raw path attachment).
acceptance_criteria: Picker shows directories first, then files, with '..' parent
Arrow keys navigate, Enter opens/attaches, Esc cancels
Selected file path inserted into input buffer

user_stories.title: /undo Command
user_stories.description:
Port the /undo command from tui_old.rs L5212-L5270. When invoked, it removes the last user message and all subsequent assistant/tool responses from both the TUI message list and the underlying Session.

Logic: Walk app messages backwards. Remove everything until you've removed the last 'user' message (inclusive). Stop if you hit a previous user message or a system message before finding a user message. Then do the same for session.messages (using Role::User, Role::System etc). Call session.save() after modification.

Show 'Nothing to undo.' if no user message found. Show 'Undid last message and response.' on success.

Add /undo to the slash command handler in src/tui/app/commands.rs and the hint list.
acceptance_criteria: /undo removes last user turn and all subsequent assistant/tool responses
Shows 'Nothing to undo.' when nothing to undo

user_stories.title: Chat Sync / MinIO Persistence
user_stories.description:
Replace the 9-line stub in src/tui/chat/sync.rs with the full MinIO chat sync system from tui_old.rs L460-L500 (config), L1073-L1130 (batch upload), L6690-L6760 (event handler).

ChatSyncConfig struct: endpoint, access_key, secret_key, bucket, prefix. ChatSyncBatch struct: messages to upload, session_id, batch_number.

sync_chat_archive_batch() — uploads a batch of messages to MinIO as JSON. run_chat_sync_worker() — background tokio task that periodically batches and uploads new messages.

ChatSyncUiEvent enum: Status(String), BatchUploaded { count, batch_num }, Error(String). handle_chat_sync_event() updates status fields on AppState.

to_archive_record() converts ChatMessage to a serializable archive record.

Add state fields: chat_sync_rx, chat_sync_status, chat_sync_last_success, chat_sync_last_error, chat_sync_uploaded_count, chat_sync_uploaded_batches.

Add chat_sync_rx branch to event loop in src/tui/app/event_loop.rs.

Constants already exist in src/tui/constants.rs (CHAT_SYNC_*).
acceptance_criteria: ChatSyncConfig struct with endpoint, credentials, bucket
ChatSyncUiEvent enum with Status, BatchUploaded, Error variants

user_stories.title: Protocol Registry View
user_stories.description:
Port the protocol registry view from tui_old.rs L9450-L9600 (render) and L8630-L8674 (ui dispatch). This is a master/detail view showing registered AgentCards from the protocol bus.

Add ViewMode::Protocol variant to models/mod.rs. Add protocol_selected (usize) and protocol_scroll (u16) fields to AppState.

render_protocol_registry() — Left pane: list of AgentCards with name, URL, status. Right pane: detail view of selected card showing full metadata, capabilities, skills.

Add Ctrl+P key binding to open protocol registry. Update /protocol command to switch to Protocol view (currently aliased to /bus which shows bus log, not registry).

Navigation: arrow keys select card, PgUp/PgDn scroll detail, Esc returns to chat.
acceptance_criteria: Left pane lists AgentCards with name, URL, status
Right pane shows selected card detail
Navigation: arrows select, PgUp/PgDn scroll, Esc back

user_stories.title: Missing Key Bindings: Ctrl+L, Ctrl+S, Ctrl+B
user_stories.description:
Add missing keyboard shortcuts from tui_old.rs L8122-L8270 to src/tui/app/event_handlers.rs.

Ctrl+L — Switch to bus protocol log (ViewMode::Bus). Same as F4 and /bus command.
Ctrl+S — Toggle swarm view (ViewMode::Swarm). Same as F2 and /swarm toggle.
Ctrl+B — Reserved for future webview layout toggle (for now, show status message 'Webview layout not yet available').

These are convenience shortcuts that mirror existing slash commands.
acceptance_criteria: Ctrl+B shows 'Webview layout not yet available' status
Key bindings documented in help overlay

user_stories.title: Message Line Caching for Performance
user_stories.description:
Port the message line caching system from tui_old.rs L6750-L6800. Currently the new code rebuilds all message Line<'static> objects every frame, which is expensive for long conversations.

Add fields to AppState: cached_message_lines (Vec<Line<'static>>), cached_messages_len (usize), cached_max_width (usize), cached_streaming_snapshot (Option<String>), cached_processing (bool).

Implement get_or_build_message_lines(theme, max_width) -> &[Line<'static>] on AppState. This returns cached lines if messages.len(), max_width, streaming text, and processing state haven't changed. Otherwise rebuilds and caches.

Update render_chat_view in src/tui/ui/main.rs to use cached lines instead of rebuilding.
acceptance_criteria: get_or_build_message_lines() returns cached lines when possible
Cache invalidated when message count changes
Cache invalidated when terminal width changes
Cache invalidated when streaming text snapshot changes

user_stories.title: Easy-Mode Command Aliases
user_stories.description:
Port the easy-mode command alias system from tui_old.rs L10750-L10850. These are user-friendly aliases that map to advanced commands.

Implement normalize_easy_command(input) -> String in src/tui/app/commands.rs or text.rs. Mappings:
- /add <name> -> /spawn <name>
- /talk <name> <msg> -> /agent <name> <msg>
- /list -> /agents
- /remove <name> -> /kill <name>
- /focus <name> -> /agent <name>
- /home -> /agent main

Call normalize_easy_command() early in the input handling pipeline before slash command dispatch.

Add these aliases to the slash command hint list so autocomplete shows them.
acceptance_criteria: Aliases appear in slash command hints

user_stories.title: Autochat Multi-Agent Relay
user_stories.description:
Port the autochat relay system from tui_old.rs L4373-L4470 (commands), L5800-L6690 (execution). This is the largest missing system (~2000 lines).

Add fields to AppState: autochat_rx (Option<mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<AutochatUiEvent>>), autochat_running (bool), autochat_started_at (Option<Instant>), autochat_status (Option<String>).

Automate multi-turn agent execution: /autochat [count] <task> launches a relay that sends the task to the agent, waits for completion, then sends follow-up prompts for the specified count.

start_autochat_execution() — spawns a background task that drives the relay. RelayCheckpoint for crash recovery — serialize current state to disk periodically so /resume can pick up.

/autochat-local forces local CUDA model. /resume [session_id] resumes from checkpoint.

Add autochat_rx branch to event loop. Constants already exist (AUTOCHAT_*).

user_stories.title: OKR Approval Gate and /go Command
user_stories.description:
Port the OKR approval gate from tui_old.rs L1280-L1360. The /go command is OKR-gated: it drafts an OKR, shows it for interactive approval (A to approve, D to decline), then starts autochat.

PendingOkrApproval struct with: okr (Okr), okr_run (OkrRun), task (String), model (String). propose() async method drafts an OKR via the model.

Add pending_okr_approval: Option<PendingOkrApproval> and okr_repository: Option<Arc<OkrRepository>> to AppState.

In event_handlers.rs, when pending_okr_approval is Some, intercept 'a' key (approve) and 'd' key (decline). On approve, start autochat with the approved OKR. On decline, clear the pending approval.

/go <task> triggers: draft OKR -> show approval UI -> on approve -> start autochat.

is_easy_go_command() and next_go_model() for model rotation between available models.
acceptance_criteria: pending_okr_approval and okr_repository fields on AppState
A key approves, D key declines

technical_requirements: All changes must compile with `cargo check` and pass `cargo clippy --all-features`
Preserve existing modular TUI architecture under src/tui/ (59 files across app/, ui/, chat/, models/, utils/)
Reference implementation is tui_old.rs (11,964 lines) at project root — port logic, not copy verbatim
Use ratatui 0.30.0 APIs and Stylize helpers per code style guide
Use tracing macros (info!, warn!, error!) instead of println!
Never hardcode secrets — all credentials from Vault
New state fields go on AppState in src/tui/app/state.rs
New slash commands go in src/tui/app/commands.rs
New key bindings go in src/tui/app/event_handlers.rs
New ViewMode variants go in src/tui/models/mod.rs
